title: Hemionitis itatiaiensis
taxonomy: Hemionitis itatiaiensis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is native to Brazil and is found in the Atlantic Forest. The classification of Hemionitis itatiaiensis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Dryopteridaceae, Genus: Hemionitis, Species: H. itatiaiensis. Hemionitis itatiaiensis is also known as the Itatiaia hemionitis. The exact classification of Hemionitis itatiaiensis may vary slightly depending on the source.
uses: Hemionitis itatiaiensis is a plant species found in the Atlantic Forest of Brazil, and it has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are used to prepare infusions and decoctions, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties.
